summary:
Only two sentences. Must focus strictly on clinically significant points related to the query.

The patient’s nail concerns suggest psoriasis due to classic features like pitting or onycholysis commonly seen in nail psoriasis. Although silvery plaques on extensor surfaces (option A) are characteristic of cutaneous psoriasis, they are more indicative of systemic disease presence than specific nail-related abnormalities—making them less direct answers to a request for an "additional" nail-associated finding.
